What's going on everybody? Sales floor decision matrix here. Finally, we are here at last. So this is, if you see you are below average on any of those prior stats, be it lead flow, scheduling, show rates, closing, backouts, all of those things I am about to break down. How to fix it. So my sales are too low. Are they though? So first thing I am going to ask if we were on the phone and you are saying hey my sales are low. Obviously as the initial benchmarks, what is your total percentage of leads closed? If you are below 15% or especially if you are below 10% we would be like alright, so then we would start digging into this. So lead flow wise, if your cost per lead is under what I said which was $20 a day, I would continue to spend up until I got 20 leads per day which is what that first line is. Spend until 20 leads per day. Alright, the next thing is if the lead cost goes above that, then I would be looking at getting new creative which means either using more of the existing testic creator that we have or when you are making your own creative making sure that it does in layless words that they don't suck. That basically means having over the top energy. You have to look like an absolute crackhead on these things to make them really be above and beyond. That is covered in the lead gin portion but I am just reiterating here. I see so many ads where I see gyms and they are like, we are looking for 30 locals to transfer their bodies in 6 weeks. You are going to get a meal plan, grocery. You can't have that kind of energy. You have to be like nuts and go crazy and do what is necessary to get the job done. Alright, next thing is just make more ads. So if you are like, okay, I have some and I can't get them to consistently convert. I always have 2 or 3 winners but I need to have 6 or 8 winners. Then you just need to be making more ads on a more regular basis. A good way to, I would say a good benchmark that I recommend for our legacy guys is just make 10 ads a week. Just like 4 hours, one day a week you dedicate to making sure that you have new ads that are done. Period. That is your responsibility as CEO, as business owner for your gym. Use tested funnel. This is kind of like, I can't tell you how many times I have seen people try and mess with the funnel. We test stuff. We test it so that it works. You are like, well, it is not converting for me and then we look and they changed a bunch of things to what they wanted it to be. One, we can no longer say it is compliant. Two, use the stuff that is tested. If you want to bear the cost of testing, then bear the cost of testing but don't. You get my point. Okay, next thing is if for some reason all those other 4 are checked off, lead ads is one way that you can run ads that tends to always be cheaper and in a lot of markets will work. The last one is, and this is really like a last, last resort, you can always use a new lead generation source. If you are on Facebook right now, switching over to Instagram is another alternative. But I would still look at the first two are going to be the issue literally 90% of the time. People are like, I need to make more sales. It is like, cool, spend more. You are getting 4 leads a day because you are spending $40 a day and you are getting $10 leads. It is like, awesome, spend $300 a day and you will make more money. Just one final thought on the spend. If you are getting $10 leads and you only put $1,000 in ads, you are only going to make $10,000. I personally would rather get $7,000 on 6 grand and make $42,000 and spend $6,000 to make $42,000 because then I am actually netting $36,000 whereas if I am getting $10,000 on $1,000 I net $9,000. So I net 4 times as much money even though I make only $7,000 versus $10,000 because I am actually willing to put money into the machine. You have to understand that. Like how much absolute money you are making, don't obsess about. Well, I got $20,000 to one, cool, you only spend $500. Who cares? Spend like you mean it and you will make money like you mean it. Okay, scheduling. So make sure that your automated scheduler is working. Sometimes there are glitches so you should test your own funnel process if you see a huge drop in scheduling rates. Make sure that it is there and that it is working. Next is speed to contact. This is huge and I have already gone over this in lead nurture process but this is the twins, the twin pillars. Speed to contact, outreach volume. If you are not contacting every lead within the first two, three minutes that is coming in between 8am and 9pm then you are leaving money on the table. Because if you can get people closer to their pain in the time of interest you are going to have higher schedule rates and significantly higher show rates which I will get to in a second. Which leads me to the last thing is actually follow the process. Do the double dial and send the text. Like your double dial is your new single dial. Like that is the new 30s, the new 20s, you get my point. Like this is the process, make sure the auto schedule is working, your speed to contact is probably not fast enough and you need to massively increase your outreach volume. I am going to say one thing real quick and then I am going to skip back to here. Alright, so this is a post that my wife made, Laila. It is a wife's perspective. Not many of you know the entire story of Alex and I but to keep you short within six weeks of meeting him. I went to work from the concept of gym lunch which he told me about on our first date. Fast forward seven weeks and we flew out to our first gym lunch location together where I was supposed to shout out to him to replicate his machine like process. What many of you don't know is how much my world is contorted and my definition of hard work is redefined. I had never been pushed so much in my life. It was anything but a honeymoon face people talk about. My point in saying this is that there are few defining monos we had together. One of which is that I had just spent two hours straight calling our entire lead list plus two to three hours of selling sprinkled in and then I stopped and I told Alex that I had called all the leads and texted them and then that is when Alex looked back at me and said, awesome, call them again, start back at the top. I legit wanted to cry. I was tired, I was annoyed and honestly wondering what did I get myself into but he laid out the facts to me upon my clear disapproval of the idea and I learned that that day forward it made no sense for me to be there with him to learn and grow if I did not follow process to the T even when I'd rather eat a cactus. After seeing the amount of real call volume that was needed, mind you, I was selling also so the days were 16, 18 hours long. I was sent out on my own gym lunch as you can see I called my butt off. I listen in on the daily calls and hear the percentages of leads to sales and I really just feel like it needs to be end files. You need to call more. You need to call until your finger's short. You can need to call until you cannot speak. There's no way to have that many unqualified or uncontacted leads if you're calling the right volume because 85% of the population is overweight. I know the numbers. I've done losses personally in 10 plus markets. If you hustle consistently, you get consistent numbers. I know mine across the board 25% lead conversion, 80% close, 87% close rate on challenge leads to sales. And just to prove to yourself how much I called, I screen tried to mask campaign blue equals scheduled. She signed up 232 people and I had 187 heart signups that actually showed up and executed the program. My point is do the hard exhausting mentally draining character testing work for less than four weeks and your world will be enormous. When we did this, we had no automated reminders. We actually did it. We didn't have automated reminders. We didn't have automated scheduling either. So when she had all these blues that had to get scheduled, she scheduled 100% of them manually and found out the time they were going to come in. So I'm going to show you these. This is what your schedule rate should look like. And mind you, she did not have automated scheduling. All right. I'm showing you this. And all these greens are sales. All right. Like all these greens are sales. Closed sales. All right. So I show you that not to discourage you, but to show you that like it is possible. And a lot of times we need to redefine what our level of effort really is. Okay. So that is scheduling. Let's dig into the show right in the next video.
